title: Unpaid Infant Care Leave (UICL)
text:
Unpaid Infant Care Leave (UICL) - SupportGoWhere
Unpaid Infant Care Leave (UICL)
Share link
Provides up to 12 days of unpaid leave for eligible parents with Singapore citizen children below 2 years old.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
12 days of unpaid leave for each parent with a Singapore citizen child below 2 years old from 1 Jan 2024
6 days of unpaid leave for each parent with a Singapore citizen child below 2 years old before 1 Jan 2024
Collapse all sections
About this support

12 days of unpaid leave per relevant period* for each parent with a Singapore citizen child below 2 years old, starting from 1 January 2024

6 days of unpaid leave per relevant period for each parent with a Singapore citizen child below 2 years old, before 1 January 2024




*Relevant period is the calendar year or any 12-month relevant period with mutual agreement between employer and employee.

If you are self-employed or if there is no mutual agreement with your employer, the 12-month relevant period will be the calendar year by default (1 Jan to 31 Dec of the year).

Who is this for?

Child is a Singapore Citizen below 2 years old

Parent has served current employer for at least 3 months

For adoptive parents, eligibility starts after the Adoption Order is passed

How to apply?

Inform your employer in advance of your intention to take UICL and get their approval.

Where can I find help?

View more details

MSFCares: 1800 111 2222 
(say ‘GPLS’ when asked for the hotline you want to reach)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 15 Dec 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
